AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the application.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group used for the application.

OpsItemSNSTopicArn.description: The SNS topic provided to Application Insights that is associated to the created opsItems to receive SNS notifications for opsItem updates.

SNSNotificationArn.description: The SNS topic ARN that is associated with SNS notifications for updates or issues.

OpsCenterEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights will create opsItems for any problem detected by Application Insights for an application.

CWEMonitorEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights can listen to CloudWatch events for the application resources, such as instance terminated, failed deployment, and others.

Remarks.description:
The issues on the user side that block Application Insights from successfully monitoring an application. Example remarks include:

  • “Configuring application, detected 1 Errors, 3 Warnings”

  • “Configuring application, detected 1 Unconfigured Components”

AutoConfigEnabled.description: Indicates whether auto-configuration is turned on for this application.

DiscoveryType.description: The method used by Application Insights to onboard your resources.

AttachMissingPermission.description: If set to true, the managed policies for SSM and CW will be attached to the instance roles if they are missing.

NextToken.description: The token used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the application.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group used for the application.

OpsItemSNSTopicArn.description: The SNS topic provided to Application Insights that is associated to the created opsItems to receive SNS notifications for opsItem updates.

SNSNotificationArn.description: The SNS topic ARN that is associated with SNS notifications for updates or issues.

OpsCenterEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights will create opsItems for any problem detected by Application Insights for an application.

CWEMonitorEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights can listen to CloudWatch events for the application resources, such as instance terminated, failed deployment, and others.

Remarks.description:
The issues on the user side that block Application Insights from successfully monitoring an application. Example remarks include:

  • “Configuring application, detected 1 Errors, 3 Warnings”

  • “Configuring application, detected 1 Unconfigured Components”

AutoConfigEnabled.description: Indicates whether auto-configuration is turned on for this application.

DiscoveryType.description: The method used by Application Insights to onboard your resources.

AttachMissingPermission.description: If set to true, the managed policies for SSM and CW will be attached to the instance roles if they are missing.

EventList.description: The list of configuration events and their corresponding details.

NextToken.description: The NextToken value to include in a future ListConfigurationHistory request. When the results of a ListConfigurationHistory request exceed MaxResults, this value can be used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group of the application to which the configuration event belongs.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the application to which the configuration event belongs.

MonitoredResourceARN.description: The resource monitored by Application Insights.

EventStatus.description: The status of the configuration update event. Possible values include INFO, WARN, and ERROR.

EventResourceType.description: The resource type that Application Insights attempted to configure, for example, CLOUDWATCH_ALARM.

EventDetail.description: The details of the event in plain text.

EventResourceName.description: The name of the resource Application Insights attempted to configure.

NextToken.description: The token used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the resource group owner.

ShortName.description: The short name of the problem associated with the SNS notification.

Insights.description: A detailed analysis of the problem using machine learning.

StartTime.description: The time when the problem started, in epoch seconds.

EndTime.description: The time when the problem ended, in epoch seconds.

SeverityLevel.description: A measure of the level of impact of the problem.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the resource group affected by the problem.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group affected by the problem.

Feedback.description: Feedback provided by the user about the problem.

RecurringCount.description: The number of times that the same problem reoccurred after the first time it was resolved.

LastRecurrenceTime.description: The last time that the problem reoccurred after its last resolution.

Visibility.description: Specifies whether or not you can view the problem. Updates to ignored problems do not generate notifications.

ResolutionMethod.description: Specifies how the problem was resolved. If the value is AUTOMATIC, the system resolved the problem. If the value is MANUAL, the user resolved the problem. If the value is UNRESOLVED, then the problem is not resolved.

WorkloadConfiguration.description: The configuration settings of the workload. The value is the escaped JSON of the configuration.

PatternSetName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 30 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

PatternName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

Pattern.description: A regular expression that defines the log pattern. A log pattern can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The pattern must be DFA compatible. Patterns that utilize forward lookahead or backreference constructions are not supported.

Rank.description: Rank of the log pattern. Must be a value between 1 and 1,000,000. The patterns are sorted by rank, so we recommend that you set your highest priority patterns with the lowest rank. A pattern of rank 1 will be the first to get matched to a log line. A pattern of rank 1,000,000 will be last to get matched. When you configure custom log patterns from the console, a Low severity pattern translates to a 750,000 rank. A Medium severity pattern translates to a 500,000 rank. And a High severity pattern translates to a 250,000 rank. Rank values less than 1 or greater than 1,000,000 are reserved for Amazon Web Services provided patterns.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the application.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group used for the application.

OpsItemSNSTopicArn.description: The SNS topic provided to Application Insights that is associated to the created opsItems to receive SNS notifications for opsItem updates.

SNSNotificationArn.description: The SNS topic ARN that is associated with SNS notifications for updates or issues.

OpsCenterEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights will create opsItems for any problem detected by Application Insights for an application.

CWEMonitorEnabled.description: Indicates whether Application Insights can listen to CloudWatch events for the application resources, such as instance terminated, failed deployment, and others.

AttachMissingPermission.description: If set to true, the managed policies for SSM and CW will be attached to the instance roles if they are missing.

ApplicationComponent.description: Describes a standalone resource or similarly grouped resources that the application is made up of.

ResourceList.description: The list of resource ARNs that belong to the component.

Monitor.description: Indicates whether the application component is monitored.

Tier.description: The tier of the application component. Supported tiers include DOT_NET_CORE, DOT_NET_WORKER, DOT_NET_WEB, SQL_SERVER, and DEFAULT

ComponentConfiguration.description: The configuration settings of the component. The value is the escaped JSON of the configuration.
The recommended configuration settings of the component. The value is the escaped JSON of the configuration.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the resource group owner.

PatternSetName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 30 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

PatternName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

Pattern.description: A regular expression that defines the log pattern. A log pattern can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The pattern must be DFA compatible. Patterns that utilize forward lookahead or backreference constructions are not supported.

Rank.description: Rank of the log pattern. Must be a value between 1 and 1,000,000. The patterns are sorted by rank, so we recommend that you set your highest priority patterns with the lowest rank. A pattern of rank 1 will be the first to get matched to a log line. A pattern of rank 1,000,000 will be last to get matched. When you configure custom log patterns from the console, a Low severity pattern translates to a 750,000 rank. A Medium severity pattern translates to a 500,000 rank. And a High severity pattern translates to a 250,000 rank. Rank values less than 1 or greater than 1,000,000 are reserved for Amazon Web Services provided patterns.

StartTime.description: The time when the observation was first detected, in epoch seconds.

EndTime.description: The time when the observation ended, in epoch seconds.

SourceARN.description: The source resource ARN of the observation.

LineTime.description: The timestamp in the CloudWatch Logs that specifies when the matched line occurred.

Unit.description: The unit of the source observation metric.

Value.description: The value of the source observation metric.

CloudWatchEventId.description: The ID of the CloudWatch Event-based observation related to the detected problem.

CloudWatchEventSource.description: The source of the CloudWatch Event.

CloudWatchEventDetailType.description: The detail type of the CloudWatch Event-based observation, for example, EC2 Instance State-change Notification.

HealthEventArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Health Event-based observation.

HealthService.description: The service to which the Health Event belongs, such as EC2.

HealthEventTypeCode.description: The type of the Health event, for example, AWS_EC2_POWER_CONNECTIVITY_ISSUE.

HealthEventTypeCategory.description: The category of the Health event, such as issue.

HealthEventDescription.description: The description of the Health event provided by the service, such as Amazon EC2.

CodeDeployDeploymentId.description: The deployment ID of the CodeDeploy-based observation related to the detected problem.

CodeDeployDeploymentGroup.description: The deployment group to which the CodeDeploy deployment belongs.

CodeDeployState.description: The status of the CodeDeploy deployment, for example SUCCESS or FAILURE.

CodeDeployApplication.description: The CodeDeploy application to which the deployment belongs.

CodeDeployInstanceGroupId.description: The instance group to which the CodeDeploy instance belongs.

Ec2State.description: The state of the instance, such as STOPPING or TERMINATING.

RdsEventCategories.description: The category of an RDS event.

RdsEventMessage.description: The message of an RDS event.

S3EventName.description: The name of the S3 CloudWatch Event-based observation.

StatesExecutionArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the step function execution-based observation.

StatesArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the step function-based observation.

StatesStatus.description: The status of the step function-related observation.

StatesInput.description: The input to the step function-based observation.

EbsEvent.description: The type of EBS CloudWatch event, such as createVolume, deleteVolume or attachVolume.

EbsResult.description: The result of an EBS CloudWatch event, such as failed or succeeded.

EbsCause.description: The cause of an EBS CloudWatch event.

EbsRequestId.description: The request ID of an EBS CloudWatch event.

XRayFaultPercent.description: The X-Ray request fault percentage for this node.

XRayThrottlePercent.description: The X-Ray request throttle percentage for this node.

XRayErrorPercent.description: The X-Ray request error percentage for this node.

XRayRequestCount.description: The X-Ray request count for this node.

XRayRequestAverageLatency.description: The X-Ray node request average latency for this node.

XRayNodeName.description: The name of the X-Ray node.

XRayNodeType.description: The type of the X-Ray node.

SNSNotificationArn.description: The SNS notification topic ARN of the problem.

ShortName.description: The short name of the problem associated with the SNS notification.

Insights.description: A detailed analysis of the problem using machine learning.

StartTime.description: The time when the problem started, in epoch seconds.

EndTime.description: The time when the problem ended, in epoch seconds.

SeverityLevel.description: A measure of the level of impact of the problem.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the owner of the resource group affected by the problem.

ResourceGroupName.description: The name of the resource group affected by the problem.

Feedback.description: Feedback provided by the user about the problem.

RecurringCount.description: The number of times that the same problem reoccurred after the first time it was resolved.

LastRecurrenceTime.description: The last time that the problem reoccurred after its last resolution.

Visibility.description: Specifies whether or not you can view the problem. Updates to ignored problems do not generate notifications.

ResolutionMethod.description: Specifies how the problem was resolved. If the value is AUTOMATIC, the system resolved the problem. If the value is MANUAL, the user resolved the problem. If the value is UNRESOLVED, then the problem is not resolved.

ObservationList.description: The list of observations related to the problem.

WorkloadRemarks.description: If logging is supported for the resource type, shows whether the component has configured logs to be monitored.

WorkloadConfiguration.description: The configuration settings of the workload. The value is the escaped JSON of the configuration.

NextToken.description: The token to request the next page of results.

ComponentRemarks.description: If logging is supported for the resource type, indicates whether the component has configured logs to be monitored.

ResourceType.description: The resource type. Supported resource types include EC2 instances, Auto Scaling group, Classic ELB, Application ELB, and SQS Queue.

OsType.description: The operating system of the component.

Tier.description: The stack tier of the application component.

Monitor.description: Indicates whether the application component is monitored.

DetectedWorkload.description: Workloads detected in the application component.

AccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the resource group owner.

NextToken.description: The token used to retrieve the next page of results. This value is null when there are no more results to return.

PatternSetName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 30 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

PatternName.description: The name of the log pattern. A log pattern name can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The characters can be Unicode letters, digits, or one of the following symbols: period, dash, underscore.

Pattern.description: A regular expression that defines the log pattern. A log pattern can contain as many as 50 characters, and it cannot be empty. The pattern must be DFA compatible. Patterns that utilize forward lookahead or backreference constructions are not supported.

Rank.description: Rank of the log pattern. Must be a value between 1 and 1,000,000. The patterns are sorted by rank, so we recommend that you set your highest priority patterns with the lowest rank. A pattern of rank 1 will be the first to get matched to a log line. A pattern of rank 1,000,000 will be last to get matched. When you configure custom log patterns from the console, a Low severity pattern translates to a 750,000 rank. A Medium severity pattern translates to a 500,000 rank. And a High severity pattern translates to a 250,000 rank. Rank values less than 1 or greater than 1,000,000 are reserved for Amazon Web Services provided patterns.

Tags.description: An array that lists all the tags that are associated with the application. Each tag consists of a required tag key (Key) and an associated tag value (Value).

Key.description: One part of a key-value pair that defines a tag. The maximum length of a tag key is 128 characters. The minimum length is 1 character.

Value.description: The optional part of a key-value pair that defines a tag. The maximum length of a tag value is 256 characters. The minimum length is 0 characters. If you don't want an application to have a specific tag value, don't specify a value for this parameter.

NextToken.description: The token to request the next page of results.

WorkloadRemarks.description: If logging is supported for the resource type, shows whether the component has configured logs to be monitored.

MissingWorkloadConfig.description: Indicates whether all of the component configurations required to monitor a workload were provided.
